title: Reinforcing Iron and Rebar Workers
description: Position and secure steel bars or mesh in concrete forms in order to reinforce concrete. Use a variety of fasteners, rod-bending machines, blowtorches, and hand tools. Includes rod busters.

tasks.text: Determine quantities, sizes, shapes, and locations of reinforcing rods from blueprints, sketches, or oral instructions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Space and fasten together rods in forms according to blueprints, using wire and pliers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Position and secure steel bars, rods, cables, or mesh in concrete forms, using fasteners, rod-bending machines, blowtorches, or hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut rods to required lengths, using metal shears, hacksaws, bar cutters, or acetylene torches.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place blocks under rebar to hold the bars off the deck when reinforcing floors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut and fit wire mesh or fabric, using hooked rods, and position fabric or mesh in concrete to reinforce concrete.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Bend steel rods with hand tools or rod-bending machines and weld them with arc-welding equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
